summary: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp in Blazor WASM because the reporter uses SkiaSharp directly in a class library without referencing SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly or SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor in the WASM app project. WASM requires static linking via NativeFileReference, which only activates when the NativeAssets.WebAssembly package is present.

stepsToReproduce: Create a Blazor WASM app that references a class library using SkiaSharp (not SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor)
Run the WASM app in the browser
Any SkiaSharp API call triggers DllNotFoundException in SKImageInfo static constructor
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows, Visual Studio, Blazor WebAssembly. SkiaSharp 3.118.0-preview.2. Works on Blazor Server.

repoLinks.description: Reporter's Blazor Captcha project using SkiaSharp in a class library

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The WASM static linking mechanism has not changed. NativeAssets.WebAssembly must still be explicitly referenced (or auto-included via Views.Blazor). Multiple commenters confirm the issue persists through 3.119.x on .NET 8/9/10.

regression.reason: Reporter claims 2.88.9 worked, but 2.x also required manual NativeFileReference setup for WASM. The packaging model changed between 2.x and 3.x. This is not a regression — WASM native assets have always required explicit setup.

analysis.summary: The SKImageInfo static constructor calls SkiaApi.sk_colortype_get_default_8888() via P/Invoke, which requires libSkiaSharp to be linked into dotnet.wasm. In Blazor WASM, native libraries are statically linked at build time using NativeFileReference items from the SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly package. The reporter uses raw SkiaSharp in a class library without Views.Blazor, so NativeAssets.WebAssembly is not auto-included. The wasm-tools workload is also required for native WASM compilation.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the DllNotFoundException is a real runtime failure, but the root cause is missing package configuration rather than a code defect. The area is libSkiaSharp.native because the failure is in native library loading/linking. Confidence on type is lower (0.70) because this could also be classified as type/question (how to configure WASM) or type/documentation (packaging docs not discoverable enough). Multiple users hitting this across .NET versions suggests the packaging UX needs improvement.

keySignals.interpretation: libSkiaSharp.a was not statically linked into dotnet.wasm — NativeAssets.WebAssembly package missing from build.
Blazor Server runs on the server with normal .NET runtime — Win32/macOS native assets work. Only WASM requires static linking.

keySignals.text: I use SkiaSharp in the project BlazorCaptcha in the file Captcha.cs
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter uses raw SkiaSharp in a class library, not SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor which auto-includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly.

keySignals.text: I was able to fix it by including the NativeAssets package
keySignals.interpretation: Explicit SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly reference resolves the issue.
wasm-tools workload is required for native WASM compilation and may not be installed.

codeInvestigation.finding: Static constructor calls SkiaApi.sk_colortype_get_default_8888() which P/Invokes into libSkiaSharp. This is the first native call, so DllNotFoundException surfaces here as TypeInitializationException.

NativeFileReference items for .a files are only included when UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly is true AND SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly package is referenced. Without this package, no native assets are linked into dotnet.wasm.

Views.Blazor auto-includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly as a project reference (when not building inside VS). This is why Views.Blazor users get native assets automatically, but raw SkiaSharp users do not.
Documentation explicitly states NativeAssets.WebAssembly must be added manually or via Views.Blazor/Views.Uno.WinUI. WASM uses static linking, not dynamic P/Invoke loading.

workarounds: Add SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly as a direct PackageReference in the Blazor WASM app project
Use SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor instead of raw SkiaSharp (auto-includes NativeAssets.WebAssembly)
Run 'dotnet workload install wasm-tools' or 'dotnet workload restore' to ensure the WASM build toolchain is installed
Ensure WasmBuildNative is not explicitly set to false in the project file
nextQuestions: Does the reporter's WASM app project reference SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly or SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor?
Is the wasm-tools workload installed on the reporter's machine?
Is WasmBuildNative set to false anywhere in the project or Directory.Build.props?

resolution.hypothesis: The WASM app project is missing SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly, so the Emscripten linker never includes libSkiaSharp.a in dotnet.wasm. The wasm-tools workload may also be missing.

proposals.title: Add NativeAssets.WebAssembly package reference
proposals.description: Add SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly as a direct PackageReference in the Blazor WASM application project (not the class library). This provides the NativeFileReference MSBuild items that link libSkiaSharp.a into dotnet.wasm at build time.

proposals.title: Install wasm-tools workload
proposals.description: Install the wasm-tools workload which provides the Emscripten toolchain needed for native WASM compilation. Without it, NativeFileReference items are ignored and libSkiaSharp is not linked.
proposals.codeSnippet:
dotnet workload install wasm-tools
# or: dotnet workload restore

proposals.title: Improve error messaging or auto-include for WASM
proposals.description: Consider auto-including NativeAssets.WebAssembly from the core SkiaSharp package when targeting browser/WASM TFM, similar to how Win32 and macOS are auto-included. This would prevent this recurring issue.

resolution.recommendedReason: Simplest fix confirmed by multiple users in #3422. Adding the explicit PackageReference to the WASM app project is the documented approach and resolves the DllNotFoundException immediately.

actionability.reason: The DllNotFoundException is caused by missing SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly package in the WASM app project. This is documented in packages.md and confirmed by multiple users in #3422. The workaround is well-established.

actions.description: Apply native loading and WASM labels
Post workaround with package reference and workload installation instructions
actions.comment:
Thanks for reporting this. The `DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp` in Blazor WASM happens because WebAssembly uses static linking — the native library must be compiled into `dotnet.wasm` at build time, unlike other platforms where it's loaded dynamically.

Here's a workaround you can use now. Add the `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly` package directly to your **Blazor WASM application project** (not the class library):

```xml
<PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly" Version="3.119.2" />
```

Also ensure the `wasm-tools` workload is installed:

```bash
dotnet workload install wasm-tools
```

And verify that `WasmBuildNative` is not set to `false` in your project file.

If you're using `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor`, this package is auto-included — but since your BlazorCaptcha library uses SkiaSharp directly (not Views.Blazor), you need the explicit reference. The native assets must be in the executable project so the Emscripten linker can find them.

See also #3422 where several users confirmed this approach resolves the issue across .NET 8–10.

actions.description: Cross-reference related Blazor WASM DllNotFoundException issues
